attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- git-commit: Commit code changes to git with careful review and detailed commit messages. Use when Claude needs to stage and commit code changes to a git repository. This skill ensures changes are reviewed with git diff before staging, excludes markdown files (except README.md), and generates comprehensive commit messages following conventional commit standards.
- code-implementation: Implement code changes with a structured workflow. Use when Claude needs to implement features, fix bugs, or make code changes in a repository. Includes repository exploration, branch creation, implementation planning, unit testing, coverage checks, documentation, and git commits. Ensures quality through testing and proper documentation.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

file.content:
x-healthcheck-defaults: &healthcheck-defaults
  interval: 30s
  timeout: 10s
  retries: 3
  start_period: 10s

x-restart-policy: &restart-policy
  restart: unless-stopped

x-logging: &logging-defaults
  logging:
    driver: json-file
    options:
      max-size: 10m
      max-file: '3'

services:

  # ==========================================================================
  # LLM Gateway Service
  # ==========================================================================

  # LLM Gateway Service (BiFrost - Production)
  # BiFrost offers 50x faster performance than LiteLLM with 11µs overhead
  # Documentation: https://docs.getbifrost.ai
  llm-gateway:
    image: maximhq/bifrost:latest
    container_name: llm-benchmark-gateway
    ports:
    - 23040:8080      # BiFrost API (OpenAI-compatible at /v1/chat/completions)
    environment:
      # LLM Provider API Keys (set in .env file)
      OPENAI_API_KEY: ${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}
      ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY:-}
      GEMINI_API_KEY: ${GEMINI_API_KEY:-}
      GROK_API_KEY: ${GROK_API_KEY:-}
      COHERE_API_KEY: ${COHERE_API_KEY:-}
      VOYAGE_API_KEY: ${VOYAGE_API_KEY:-}
      OPENROUTER_API_KEY: ${OPENROUTER_API_KEY:-}
      # BiFrost Configuration
      APP_PORT: '8080'
      APP_HOST: 0.0.0.0
      LOG_LEVEL: ${LOG_LEVEL:-info}
      LOG_STYLE: json
    volumes:
    - ./data/bifrost:/app/data
    - ./data/bifrost/config.json:/app/data/config.json:ro
    networks:
    - llm-benchmark-network
    healthcheck:
      <<: *healthcheck-defaults
      test: [CMD, wget, --no-verbose, --tries=1, --spider, http://localhost:8080/health]
    <<: [*restart-policy, *logging-defaults]

  # ==========================================================================
  # Application Services
  # ==========================================================================

  


# =============================================================================
# Volumes
# =============================================================================



# =============================================================================
# Networks
# =============================================================================

networks:
  llm-benchmark-network:
    name: llm-benchmark-network
    driver: bridge
    ipam:
      config:
      - subnet: 172.32.0.0/16

The compose ran successfully. Here's a summary of what's up:

**Started:**
- `llm-benchmark-gateway` — BiFrost LLM gateway, OpenAI-compatible API on port `23040` (maps to internal `:8080`)

**Warnings (not errors):** The orphan container warnings are from a separate Docker Compose project (`chatbot-*` containers) sharing the same Docker daemon. They don't affect your deployment.

**Next steps to verify:**
- Check the gateway is healthy: `docker inspect llm-benchmark-gateway --format='{{.State.Health.Status}}'`
- Test the endpoint: `curl http://localhost:23040/health`
- Make sure your `.env` file has the required API keys (`OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, etc.) and the BiFrost config is at `deployments/local/data/bifrost/config.json`

Is there something specific you'd like to configure or add to this deployment (e.g., connecting a vLLM service, adding monitoring)?

aiTitle: Start Docker Compose deployment for AI agent

content.text:
Confirmed — only `deployments/local/docker-compose.yml` references BiFrost. No existing Python integration uses it. Here is the structured report.

---

# BiFrost Gateway Deployment — Research Report

## 1. Verbatim file contents

### `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/deployments/local/data/bifrost/config.json`

```json
{
    "$schema": "https://www.getbifrost.ai/schema",
    "client": {
        "drop_excess_requests": false,
        "allowed_origins": ["*"],
        "enable_logging": true
    },
    "providers": {
        "openai": {
            "keys": [
                {
                    "name": "openai-primary",
                    "value": "env.OPENAI_API_KEY",
                    "models": [
                        "gpt-5",
                        "gpt-5-mini",
                        "gpt-5-nano",
                        "gpt-5.2"
                    ],
                    "weight": 1.0
                }
            ]
        },
        "anthropic": {
            "keys": [
                {
                    "name": "anthropic-primary",
                    "value": "env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY",
                    "models": [
                        "claude-opus-4-6",
                        "claude-sonnet-4-6",
                        "claude-haiku-4-5"
                    ],
                    "weight": 1.0
                }
            ],
            "network_config": {
                "default_request_timeout_in_seconds": 300
            }
        },
        "gemini": {
            "keys": [
                {
                    "name": "gemini-primary",
                    "value": "env.GEMINI_API_KEY",
                    "models": [
                        "gemini-3.1-pro-preview",
                        "gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview",
                        "gemini-3-flash-preview",
                        "gemini-2.5-pro",
                        "gemini-2.5-flash",
                        "gemini-2.5-flash-lite"
                    ],
                    "weight": 1.0
                }
            ]
        }
    },
    "config_store": {
        "enabled": true,
        "type": "sqlite",
        "config": { "path": "/app/data/config.db" }
    },
    "logs_store": {
        "enabled": true,
        "type": "sqlite",
        "config": { "path": "/app/data/logs.db" }
    }
}
```

Note: only `openai`, `anthropic`, and `gemini` are wired in `config.json`, even though docker-compose passes through `GROK_API_KEY`, `COHERE_API_KEY`, `VOYAGE_API_KEY`, `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` environment variables. Those four providers are NOT declared in the live config.

### `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/deployments/local/docker-compose.yml`

(Full content shown verbatim — 79 lines.) Key fields:
- `image: maximhq/bifrost:latest`
- `container_name: llm-benchmark-gateway`
- `ports: - 23040:8080`  — host 23040 maps to container 8080
- environment passes through 7 provider env vars: `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `GROK_API_KEY`, `COHERE_API_KEY`, `VOYAGE_API_KEY`, `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` (plus `APP_PORT=8080`, `APP_HOST=0.0.0.0`, `LOG_LEVEL`, `LOG_STYLE=json`)
- volumes: `./data/bifrost:/app/data` and `./data/bifrost/config.json:/app/data/config.json:ro`
- healthcheck: `wget --spider http://localhost:8080/health`
- network: bridge `llm-benchmark-network` on subnet `172.32.0.0/16`

The compose comment at line 30 explicitly states: `# BiFrost API (OpenAI-compatible at /v1/chat/completions)`.

### `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/deployments/local/.env`

```
REDACTED
REDACTED
REDACTED
```

(I will not echo full secrets in any plan/output beyond this; flagging that the deployment-local `.env` contains real-looking, unrotated keys — this is a credential-leakage concern. Only OPENAI/ANTHROPIC/GEMINI are populated; GROK/COHERE/VOYAGE/OPENROUTER are unset.)

### `.env.example` / README / template files

- No `deployments/local/.env.example`, `config.json.example`, `config.template.json`, or README exists in `deployments/local/`. Only `.env`, `docker-compose.yml`, and `data/bifrost/{config.json, config.db*, logs.db*}`.
- Repo-root `.env.example` (`/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/.env.example`) is unrelated to BiFrost — it is for teacher-model API keys used directly:
  ```
  GEMINI_API_KEY=
  OPENAI_API_KEY=
  ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=
  HF_TOKEN=
  ```
- No README or markdown docs exist anywhere under `deployments/`.

## 2. Provider + model inventory (verbatim from `config.json`)

| Provider key | Auth env var | Models (verbatim strings) |
|---|---|---|
| `openai` | `OPENAI_API_KEY` | `gpt-5`, `gpt-5-mini`, `gpt-5-nano`, `gpt-5.2` |
| `anthropic` | `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | `claude-opus-4-6`, `claude-sonnet-4-6`, `claude-haiku-4-5` |
| `gemini` | `GEMINI_API_KEY` | `gemini-3.1-pro-preview`, `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview`, `gemini-3-flash-preview`, `gemini-2.5-pro`, `gemini-2.5-flash`, `gemini-2.5-flash-lite` |

Total: **13 frontier model strings**. Providers explicitly mentioned in the user's question but NOT in `config.json`: `grok`, `cohere`, `voyage`, `openrouter` — env vars are plumbed through docker-compose but no `providers.<name>` blocks exist, so requests routed to those providers will currently fail until `config.json` is extended.

Important: BiFrost expects model strings as plain names (e.g. `gpt-5`), not provider-prefixed (`openai/gpt-5`). The `keys[].models` array binds a model to a provider entry so the gateway can route by model name.

## 3. Endpoint surface

- **Host URL from outside the container**: `http://localhost:23040` (or `http://<docker-host>:23040`)
- **Container-internal URL** (other compose services on `llm-benchmark-network`): `http://llm-gateway:8080`
- **OpenAI-compatible chat path**: `POST /v1/chat/completions` (asserted by line 30 comment in docker-compose.yml)
- **Health probe**: `GET /health` (used by container healthcheck)
- **Models listing**: `GET /v1/models` is the standard OpenAI-compatible endpoint and BiFrost implements it per its public docs at https://docs.getbifrost.ai (I was not able to fetch the URL from this sandbox, but BiFrost's drop-in OpenAI compatibility is the documented contract; assume `/v1/models`, `/v1/chat/completions`, `/v1/embeddings`).
- **Auth requirement on the gateway itself**: **None enforced.** `config.json.client` has no `auth`, no `virtual_keys`, no `governance` block. Origin policy is `"allowed_origins": ["*"]`. Provider auth happens server-side via `value: "env.OPENAI_API_KEY"` indirection; the client just calls `/v1/chat/completions` with any (or no) `Authorization` header. The OpenAI SDK pattern in this repo (`api_key="unused"`) will work as-is.

## 4. BiFrost `config.json` schema (as inferred from this file + `$schema` URL)

- Top-level keys: `client`, `providers`, `config_store`, `logs_store`.
- `client.drop_excess_requests` (bool), `client.allowed_origins` (list), `client.enable_logging` (bool).
- `providers.<provider_name>.keys[]` — each entry: `name`, `value` (either literal or `env.<VAR>`), `models[]`, `weight` (float, used for weighted load-balancing across keys).
- Optional per-provider `network_config.default_request_timeout_in_seconds`.
- `config_store` and `logs_store` both support `type: sqlite` with `config.path`.
- Schema URL declared: `https://www.getbifrost.ai/schema` — fetch this for the authoritative JSON Schema if extending the file (not retrievable from this sandbox).

To add `grok`, `cohere`, `voyage`, `openrouter`: replicate the `openai` block with the relevant env-var indirection and a `models` list.

## 5. Frontier-model name strings (one place, copy-pasteable)

```
gpt-5
gpt-5-mini
gpt-5-nano
gpt-5.2
claude-opus-4-6
claude-sonnet-4-6
claude-haiku-4-5
gemini-3.1-pro-preview
gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview
gemini-3-flash-preview
gemini-2.5-pro
gemini-2.5-flash
gemini-2.5-flash-lite
```

## 6. Auth details

- Gateway client-side auth: **none**. No virtual keys configured. Anyone with TCP access to `localhost:23040` can call `/v1/chat/completions`.
- Provider-side auth: lifted from container env (`OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`) sourced from `deployments/local/.env`. The four extra env vars in `docker-compose.yml` (`GROK_API_KEY`, `COHERE_API_KEY`, `VOYAGE_API_KEY`, `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`) are passed in but unused because the corresponding `providers` blocks are absent.
- Security note: `deployments/local/.env` contains apparent live API keys; recommend rotation before publishing.

## 7. Pre-existing integration points in the codebase

**Conclusion: there is NO existing wiring between this repo and the BiFrost gateway.** Search results:

- The string `bifrost` appears only in `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/deployments/local/docker-compose.yml`.
- The port `23040` appears only in `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/deployments/local/docker-compose.yml`.
- `BIFROST*`, `GATEWAY*`, `gateway_url` env vars do not appear anywhere in `src/`, `configs/`, `scripts/`, `serving/`, `tests/`.
- "frontier" hits in the repo refer to **Pareto frontier** (multi-objective optimization), not frontier-model benchmarking.

However, the repo already has natural seams to plug a gateway into:

- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/src/llm_workflow_agents/data/_teacher_client.py` — currently uses provider-native SDKs (`openai`, `anthropic`, `google.genai`) keyed off model-name prefixes (`gemini-*`, `gpt-*`, `claude-*`). A gateway-aware refactor would point the OpenAI client at `http://localhost:23040/v1` and route every model through it (BiFrost normalizes the request shape to OpenAI-compatible).
- vLLM-targeting `base_url` parameters defaulting to `http://localhost:8000/v1` exist in many eval/serving modules — these are sibling patterns, not gateway clients:
  - `src/llm_workflow_agents/serving/benchmark_e2e.py:128` (`base_url`)
  - `src/llm_workflow_agents/serving/orchestrator.py:62` (`self.base_url`)
  - `src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/intent_classification.py:306`
  - `src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/concurrency_benchmark.py:327`
  - `src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/longbench.py:180,249`
  - `src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/needle_haystack.py:111`
  - `src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/constrained_decoding.py:126`
  - `src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/perplexity.py:78,155`
  All instantiate `openai.OpenAI(base_url=..., api_key="unused")`, which is exactly the call signature BiFrost expects. A frontier-model benchmark could reuse these helpers verbatim by passing `base_url="http://localhost:23040/v1"` and the `model=` strings listed in section 5.

## File-path reference (all absolute)

- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/deployments/local/docker-compose.yml`
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/deployments/local/.env`

Confirmed — only `deployments/local/docker-compose.yml` references BiFrost. No existing Python integration uses it. Here is the structured report.

---

# BiFrost Gateway Deployment — Research Report

## 1. Verbatim file contents

### `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/deployments/local/data/bifrost/config.json`

```json
{
    "$schema": "https://www.getbifrost.ai/schema",
    "client": {
        "drop_excess_requests": false,
        "allowed_origins": ["*"],
        "enable_logging": true
    },
    "providers": {
        "openai": {
            "keys": [
                {
                    "name": "openai-primary",
                    "value": "env.OPENAI_API_KEY",
                    "models": [
                        "gpt-5",
                        "gpt-5-mini",
                        "gpt-5-nano",
                        "gpt-5.2"
                    ],
                    "weight": 1.0
                }
            ]
        },
        "anthropic": {
            "keys": [
                {
                    "name": "anthropic-primary",
                    "value": "env.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY",
                    "models": [
                        "claude-opus-4-6",
                        "claude-sonnet-4-6",
                        "claude-haiku-4-5"
                    ],
                    "weight": 1.0
                }
            ],
            "network_config": {
                "default_request_timeout_in_seconds": 300
            }
        },
        "gemini": {
            "keys": [
                {
                    "name": "gemini-primary",
                    "value": "env.GEMINI_API_KEY",
                    "models": [
                        "gemini-3.1-pro-preview",
                        "gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview",
                        "gemini-3-flash-preview",
                        "gemini-2.5-pro",
                        "gemini-2.5-flash",
                        "gemini-2.5-flash-lite"
                    ],
                    "weight": 1.0
                }
            ]
        }
    },
    "config_store": {
        "enabled": true,
        "type": "sqlite",
        "config": { "path": "/app/data/config.db" }
    },
    "logs_store": {
        "enabled": true,
        "type": "sqlite",
        "config": { "path": "/app/data/logs.db" }
    }
}
```

Note: only `openai`, `anthropic`, and `gemini` are wired in `config.json`, even though docker-compose passes through `GROK_API_KEY`, `COHERE_API_KEY`, `VOYAGE_API_KEY`, `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` environment variables. Those four providers are NOT declared in the live config.

### `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/deployments/local/docker-compose.yml`

(Full content shown verbatim — 79 lines.) Key fields:
- `image: maximhq/bifrost:latest`
- `container_name: llm-benchmark-gateway`
- `ports: - 23040:8080`  — host 23040 maps to container 8080
- environment passes through 7 provider env vars: `OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`, `GROK_API_KEY`, `COHERE_API_KEY`, `VOYAGE_API_KEY`, `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` (plus `APP_PORT=8080`, `APP_HOST=0.0.0.0`, `LOG_LEVEL`, `LOG_STYLE=json`)
- volumes: `./data/bifrost:/app/data` and `./data/bifrost/config.json:/app/data/config.json:ro`
- healthcheck: `wget --spider http://localhost:8080/health`
- network: bridge `llm-benchmark-network` on subnet `172.32.0.0/16`

The compose comment at line 30 explicitly states: `# BiFrost API (OpenAI-compatible at /v1/chat/completions)`.

### `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/deployments/local/.env`

```
REDACTED
REDACTED
REDACTED
```

(I will not echo full secrets in any plan/output beyond this; flagging that the deployment-local `.env` contains real-looking, unrotated keys — this is a credential-leakage concern. Only OPENAI/ANTHROPIC/GEMINI are populated; GROK/COHERE/VOYAGE/OPENROUTER are unset.)

### `.env.example` / README / template files

- No `deployments/local/.env.example`, `config.json.example`, `config.template.json`, or README exists in `deployments/local/`. Only `.env`, `docker-compose.yml`, and `data/bifrost/{config.json, config.db*, logs.db*}`.
- Repo-root `.env.example` (`/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/.env.example`) is unrelated to BiFrost — it is for teacher-model API keys used directly:
  ```
  GEMINI_API_KEY=
  OPENAI_API_KEY=
  ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=
  HF_TOKEN=
  ```
- No README or markdown docs exist anywhere under `deployments/`.

## 2. Provider + model inventory (verbatim from `config.json`)

| Provider key | Auth env var | Models (verbatim strings) |
|---|---|---|
| `openai` | `OPENAI_API_KEY` | `gpt-5`, `gpt-5-mini`, `gpt-5-nano`, `gpt-5.2` |
| `anthropic` | `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | `claude-opus-4-6`, `claude-sonnet-4-6`, `claude-haiku-4-5` |
| `gemini` | `GEMINI_API_KEY` | `gemini-3.1-pro-preview`, `gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview`, `gemini-3-flash-preview`, `gemini-2.5-pro`, `gemini-2.5-flash`, `gemini-2.5-flash-lite` |

Total: **13 frontier model strings**. Providers explicitly mentioned in the user's question but NOT in `config.json`: `grok`, `cohere`, `voyage`, `openrouter` — env vars are plumbed through docker-compose but no `providers.<name>` blocks exist, so requests routed to those providers will currently fail until `config.json` is extended.

Important: BiFrost expects model strings as plain names (e.g. `gpt-5`), not provider-prefixed (`openai/gpt-5`). The `keys[].models` array binds a model to a provider entry so the gateway can route by model name.

## 3. Endpoint surface

- **Host URL from outside the container**: `http://localhost:23040` (or `http://<docker-host>:23040`)
- **Container-internal URL** (other compose services on `llm-benchmark-network`): `http://llm-gateway:8080`
- **OpenAI-compatible chat path**: `POST /v1/chat/completions` (asserted by line 30 comment in docker-compose.yml)
- **Health probe**: `GET /health` (used by container healthcheck)
- **Models listing**: `GET /v1/models` is the standard OpenAI-compatible endpoint and BiFrost implements it per its public docs at https://docs.getbifrost.ai (I was not able to fetch the URL from this sandbox, but BiFrost's drop-in OpenAI compatibility is the documented contract; assume `/v1/models`, `/v1/chat/completions`, `/v1/embeddings`).
- **Auth requirement on the gateway itself**: **None enforced.** `config.json.client` has no `auth`, no `virtual_keys`, no `governance` block. Origin policy is `"allowed_origins": ["*"]`. Provider auth happens server-side via `value: "env.OPENAI_API_KEY"` indirection; the client just calls `/v1/chat/completions` with any (or no) `Authorization` header. The OpenAI SDK pattern in this repo (`api_key="unused"`) will work as-is.

## 4. BiFrost `config.json` schema (as inferred from this file + `$schema` URL)

- Top-level keys: `client`, `providers`, `config_store`, `logs_store`.
- `client.drop_excess_requests` (bool), `client.allowed_origins` (list), `client.enable_logging` (bool).
- `providers.<provider_name>.keys[]` — each entry: `name`, `value` (either literal or `env.<VAR>`), `models[]`, `weight` (float, used for weighted load-balancing across keys).
- Optional per-provider `network_config.default_request_timeout_in_seconds`.
- `config_store` and `logs_store` both support `type: sqlite` with `config.path`.
- Schema URL declared: `https://www.getbifrost.ai/schema` — fetch this for the authoritative JSON Schema if extending the file (not retrievable from this sandbox).

To add `grok`, `cohere`, `voyage`, `openrouter`: replicate the `openai` block with the relevant env-var indirection and a `models` list.

## 5. Frontier-model name strings (one place, copy-pasteable)

```
gpt-5
gpt-5-mini
gpt-5-nano
gpt-5.2
claude-opus-4-6
claude-sonnet-4-6
claude-haiku-4-5
gemini-3.1-pro-preview
gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview
gemini-3-flash-preview
gemini-2.5-pro
gemini-2.5-flash
gemini-2.5-flash-lite
```

## 6. Auth details

- Gateway client-side auth: **none**. No virtual keys configured. Anyone with TCP access to `localhost:23040` can call `/v1/chat/completions`.
- Provider-side auth: lifted from container env (`OPENAI_API_KEY`, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `GEMINI_API_KEY`) sourced from `deployments/local/.env`. The four extra env vars in `docker-compose.yml` (`GROK_API_KEY`, `COHERE_API_KEY`, `VOYAGE_API_KEY`, `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`) are passed in but unused because the corresponding `providers` blocks are absent.
- Security note: `deployments/local/.env` contains apparent live API keys; recommend rotation before publishing.

## 7. Pre-existing integration points in the codebase

**Conclusion: there is NO existing wiring between this repo and the BiFrost gateway.** Search results:

- The string `bifrost` appears only in `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/deployments/local/docker-compose.yml`.
- The port `23040` appears only in `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/deployments/local/docker-compose.yml`.
- `BIFROST*`, `GATEWAY*`, `gateway_url` env vars do not appear anywhere in `src/`, `configs/`, `scripts/`, `serving/`, `tests/`.
- "frontier" hits in the repo refer to **Pareto frontier** (multi-objective optimization), not frontier-model benchmarking.

However, the repo already has natural seams to plug a gateway into:

- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/src/llm_workflow_agents/data/_teacher_client.py` — currently uses provider-native SDKs (`openai`, `anthropic`, `google.genai`) keyed off model-name prefixes (`gemini-*`, `gpt-*`, `claude-*`). A gateway-aware refactor would point the OpenAI client at `http://localhost:23040/v1` and route every model through it (BiFrost normalizes the request shape to OpenAI-compatible).
- vLLM-targeting `base_url` parameters defaulting to `http://localhost:8000/v1` exist in many eval/serving modules — these are sibling patterns, not gateway clients:
  - `src/llm_workflow_agents/serving/benchmark_e2e.py:128` (`base_url`)
  - `src/llm_workflow_agents/serving/orchestrator.py:62` (`self.base_url`)
  - `src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/intent_classification.py:306`
  - `src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/concurrency_benchmark.py:327`
  - `src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/longbench.py:180,249`
  - `src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/needle_haystack.py:111`
  - `src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/constrained_decoding.py:126`
  - `src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/perplexity.py:78,155`
  All instantiate `openai.OpenAI(base_url=..., api_key="unused")`, which is exactly the call signature BiFrost expects. A frontier-model benchmark could reuse these helpers verbatim by passing `base_url="http://localhost:23040/v1"` and the `model=` strings listed in section 5.

## File-path reference (all absolute)

- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/deployments/local/docker-compose.yml`
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/deployments/local/.env`
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/deployments/local/data/bifrost/config.json`
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/deployments/local/data/bifrost/config.db` (sqlite — BiFrost runtime config store)
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/deployments/local/data/bifrost/logs.db` (sqlite — BiFrost request logs)
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/.env.example` (repo-root teacher keys — unrelated to gateway)
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/.env` (repo-root teacher keys — unrelated to gateway)
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/src/llm_workflow_agents/data/_teacher_client.py` (existing direct-SDK teacher caller — natural integration target)
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/src/llm_workflow_agents/serving/orchestrator.py` (already takes `base_url`)
- `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/src/llm_workflow_agents/serving/benchmark_e2e.py` (already takes `base_url`)

No `config.json.example`, no `config.template.json`, no `bifrost.json`, and no README/docs in `deployments/local/` exist anywhere in the repo.

I have enough information to compile the report.

# Phase 1 Benchmark Harness Research Report

## 1. File contents (verbatim)

### 1a. `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/scripts/run_exp_a_single.sh`
Already shown in full above (151 lines). Key shape:
- Lines 1-29: usage banner / arg parsing scaffold
- Lines 31-67: positional config arg + flag parsing (`--kv-cache-dtype`, `--data`, `--max-samples`, `--max-model-len`, `--max-num-seqs`, `--dry-run`); resolves config to absolute path
- Lines 69-81: inline-Python YAML readers extracting `model.name` and `inference.stochastic_trials`
- Lines 97-103: builds vLLM launch arg array
- Lines 112-135: `bash $LAUNCH_SCRIPT … &` then polls `http://localhost:8000/health` for up to 900s; HARDCODED to localhost:8000 for the readiness gate
- Lines 138-147: invokes `python3 -m llm_workflow_agents.eval.agent_benchmark` with `--model`, `--kv-cache-dtype`, `--output`, `--data`, `--max-samples`, `--stochastic-trials`, `--log-level DEBUG`. **Does NOT pass `--endpoint`** — relies on the harness default `http://localhost:8000`. Does NOT pass an API key.

### 1b. `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/scripts/run_exp_a.sh`
Already shown in full above (126 lines). Same structure, but loops over a hardcoded list of 10 YAMLs in `MODEL_CONFIGS=(…)` (lines 43-54) and only forwards `--kv-cache-dtype` to the launch script (line 89). Also does NOT forward `--endpoint`.

No other `run_exp_a*.sh` siblings exist (only those two).

### 1c. `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/agent_benchmark.py`
Already shown in full above (720 lines). Critical zones:
- Lines 33-59: `WorkflowQualityMetrics` dataclass
- Lines 62-201: pure metric-computation helpers (no I/O)
- Lines 208-220: `_load_samples` reads JSONL files
- Lines 223-354: `_call_vllm(endpoint, model, messages, temperature, max_tokens, tools, enable_thinking)` — the **single I/O surface** to the model. Hand-rolled `urllib.request` POST to `{endpoint}/v1/chat/completions` with `stream=True`. Parses SSE deltas, accumulates tool_call deltas keyed by `index`, measures TTFT and end-to-end latency, appends `<tool_call>{...}</tool_call>` tags into the returned content for downstream regex parsing
- Lines 357-481: `_replay_conversation(endpoint, model, sample, temperature, enable_thinking)` — replays a conversation with substituted assistant turns; preserves ground-truth tool responses; assigns `tool_call_id` from the model's prior tool_call list
- Lines 484-720: `if __name__ == "__main__":` CLI block

### Key CLI args (lines 507-553)
- `--model` (required) — string passed verbatim into `request_body["model"]`
- `--output` (required) — path
- `--data` — default `data/output/benchmark/task_a`
- `--endpoint` — default `http://localhost:8000` (line 517-519). This is the ONLY existing knob for routing.
- `--max-samples` int default 0
- `--stochastic-trials` int default 5
- `--kv-cache-dtype` string, only used for result metadata (recorded in JSON, NOT forwarded into requests)
- `--log-level` choice
- `--enable-thinking` bool flag

There is no `--api-key`, `--provider`, `--base-url`, `--tool-call-parser`, or `--chat-template` CLI arg.

## 2. How the harness obtains a model client

- The harness does **not** spawn vLLM. The shell script (`run_exp_a_single.sh:112`) backgrounds `serving/launch_vllm.sh`, polls `/health`, then invokes the Python harness.
- The Python harness opens an HTTP connection on every assistant turn via raw `urllib.request` (lines 251-353). It hits the **OpenAI-compatible** `/v1/chat/completions` route exposed by `vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server`.
- It is **synchronous and streaming** (SSE) — `stream=True` body (line 260), single-thread loop reading line-by-line. No `asyncio`, no `openai` SDK, no `requests`, no auth headers.
- Hardcoded request shape: `model`, `messages`, `temperature`, `max_tokens`, `stream`, `chat_template_kwargs={enable_thinking: bool}`, optional `tools`. **No `Authorization` header is ever set** (line 274 only sets `Content-Type`).

## 3. Where model name / base URL / API key / tool_call_parser / chat_template are wired in

| Setting | Where it lives | How it reaches the model |
|---|---|---|
| `model.name` | YAML model config (e.g. `configs/models_exp_a/gemma3_27b.yaml:2`) | Read by `run_exp_a_single.sh:69-74` via inline Python; passed as `--model` to `agent_benchmark.py` (`run_exp_a_single.sh:140`); placed verbatim into the request body at `agent_benchmark.py:257` |
| Base URL | NOT in any YAML. Only `--endpoint` CLI arg (`agent_benchmark.py:516-519`) defaults to `http://localhost:8000`. The shell script never overrides this. The vLLM port itself can be set via `serving.port` YAML key (`launch_vllm.sh:74`) or `--port` flag, but the eval harness has no symmetric override and is hardwired to `localhost:8000` |
| API key | **NOT WIRED ANYWHERE** in this code path. `_call_vllm` (`agent_benchmark.py:271-276`) sets only `Content-Type: application/json`. `dotenv.load_dotenv()` is called at `agent_benchmark.py:492` but no env var is consumed |
| `tool_call_parser` | YAML `serving.tool_call_parser` (e.g. `gemma3_27b.yaml:13`); read by `launch_vllm.sh:69`; passed to vLLM as `--tool-call-parser` (`launch_vllm.sh:121`). Server-side only; the harness never sees it |
| `chat_template` | YAML `serving.chat_template` (e.g. `gemma3_27b.yaml:14`, `qwen3_32b.yaml:14`). **NEVER consumed.** No `grep -rn chat_template` produced any read of this key — `launch_vllm.sh` doesn't reference it, vLLM uses the tokenizer's built-in template by default. It is purely metadata |
| `enable_thinking` | Only the Python harness CLI flag `--enable-thinking` (`agent_benchmark.py:544-552`) → `chat_template_kwargs` body field (`agent_benchmark.py:265`). Not in YAML |
| Other vLLM serving knobs (`gpu_memory_utilization`, `max_model_len`, `enforce_eager`, `max_num_seqs`, `hf_overrides`) | YAML `serving.*` → `launch_vllm.sh:67-87` → server CLI args. Server-side only |
| `extra_flags` (Phase 1B configs only) | Listed in `configs/models/cat_a/qwen35_35b_a3b.yaml:19-21` and Qwen3.6 configs. NOT consumed by `serving/launch_vllm.sh` (no grep hits in launch script) — these YAMLs appear to use a different, unimplemented serving wrapper |
| `revision` (cat_a configs) | Listed in `configs/models/cat_a/qwen3_32b.yaml:3` etc. NOT consumed by `serving/launch_vllm.sh` |

## 4. Phase 1 YAML config schemas

Two parallel directory trees, with subtly different shapes:

### `configs/models_exp_a/*.yaml` (used by `run_exp_a*.sh`)
Representative — `configs/models_exp_a/gemma3_27b.yaml` (full, 28 lines):
```yaml
model:
  name: "google/gemma-3-27b-it"
  family: "gemma"
  architecture: "dense_gqa_transformer"
  params_total: 27_000_000_000
  params_active: 27_000_000_000
  context_length: 131072
  precision: "bfloat16"
  vram_estimate_gb: 54

serving:
  engine: "vllm"
  tool_call_parser: "pythonic"
  chat_template: "gemma"
  gpu_memory_utilization: 0.90
  max_model_len: 8192
  enforce_eager: false
  hf_overrides:
    rope_scaling: null

inference:
  temperature_deterministic: 0.0
  temperature_stochastic: 0.7
  stochastic_trials: 5
  max_tokens: 2048
```

`configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b.yaml` (full, 24 lines): same shape, no `hf_overrides`, `chat_template: "chatml"`, `tool_call_parser: "hermes"`.

### `configs/models/cat_a/*.yaml` (newer / not directly wired into `run_exp_a.sh`)
`configs/models/cat_a/qwen3_32b.yaml` (full, 23 lines):
```yaml
model:
  name: "Qwen/Qwen3-32B"
  revision: "9216db5781bf21249d130ec9da846c4624c16137"
  family: "qwen"
  …
serving:
  engine: "vllm"
  tool_call_parser: "hermes"
  chat_template: "chatml"
  gpu_memory_utilization: 0.90
  max_model_len: 8192
category: "A"
benchmark_tasks:
  - "task_a"
```

`configs/models/cat_a/qwen35_35b_a3b.yaml:12-21` (extended schema with `extra_flags` and `reasoning_parser`):
```yaml
serving:
  engine: "vllm"
  tool_call_parser: "qwen3_coder"
  reasoning_parser: "qwen3"
  chat_template: "chatml"
  gpu_memory_utilization: 0.90
  max_model_len: 8192
  extra_flags:
    - "--enable-auto-tool-choice"
    - "--language-model-only"
```

The Pydantic schema for these YAMLs lives at `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/src/llm_workflow_agents/config/schema.py:44-79`. `ServingConfig` (lines 44-52) only covers: `engine`, `tool_call_parser`, `chat_template`, `gpu_memory_utilization`, `max_model_len`, `enforce_eager`. `reasoning_parser`, `extra_flags`, `revision`, `hf_overrides` are NOT in the Pydantic model — they would either be silently dropped or rejected on validation (by default Pydantic BaseModel allows extras only if not configured; these models don't set `model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")`, so extras pass silently).

Loader: `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/src/llm_workflow_agents/config/loader.py:25-33` `load_model_config` — the harness does NOT use this loader; the shell script reads YAML inline.

## 5. Existing remote / OpenAI-compatible / frontier code paths

| Path | File:lines | Mode | Reusable for frontier API? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Hand-rolled urllib SSE → `{endpoint}/v1/chat/completions` (Phase 1 agent benchmark) | `eval/agent_benchmark.py:223-354` | sync streaming, no auth | The TARGET of the question. Currently ONLY supports unauth localhost vLLM |
| Hand-rolled urllib SSE for concurrency probing | `eval/concurrency_benchmark.py:185-248` (with `--base-url` CLI arg at line 488) | async via thread-pool, no auth | Pattern, but no auth |
| `openai.OpenAI(base_url=…, api_key="unused")` for needle-in-haystack | `eval/needle_haystack.py:111, 125, 132` | sync via openai SDK | Already uses the openai SDK pattern. Hardcoded `api_key="unused"` |
| `openai.OpenAI(base_url=…, api_key="unused")` for LongBench | `eval/longbench.py:180, 255, 263` | sync via openai SDK | Same pattern as needle |
| `openai.OpenAI(base_url=…, api_key="unused")` for constrained-decoding eval | `eval/constrained_decoding.py:126, 149` | sync via openai SDK | Same pattern |
| `openai.OpenAI(base_url=…, api_key="unused")` for intent classification | `eval/intent_classification.py:306, 354` | sync via openai SDK | Same pattern |
| `openai.OpenAI(base_url=…, api_key="unused")` for perplexity-vllm | `eval/perplexity.py:78, 155` | sync via openai SDK | Same pattern |
| `openai.OpenAI(base_url=…, api_key="unused")` for quant_benchmark | `eval/quant_benchmark.py:255` | sync via openai SDK | Same pattern |
| `openai.AsyncOpenAI(base_url=…, api_key="unused")` for E2E benchmark | `serving/benchmark_e2e.py:128, 156, 158` | async openai SDK | Same pattern, async |
| `openai.AsyncOpenAI(base_url=…, api_key="unused")` for orchestrator | `serving/orchestrator.py:62, 132, 134` | async openai SDK | Same pattern, async; `base_url` taken from `orchestrator_config["base_url"]` (configurable) |
| Frontier-provider teacher client (data generation, NOT eval) | `data/_teacher_client.py:1-94` | sync; routes by model-name prefix to `openai.OpenAI`, `anthropic.Anthropic`, or `google.genai`; reads `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` from env via `dotenv` | **Closest existing analogue.** Already implements provider routing pattern, but: (a) no streaming, (b) no `tools`/`tool_calls` support, (c) returns plain text only, not OpenAI message objects |

**Findings on existing remote/openai-compatible pathways**

- The codebase already standardises on the OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions wire format almost everywhere except `agent_benchmark.py` (which uses raw urllib).
- 8 different eval modules use `openai.OpenAI(base_url=…, api_key="unused")` and accept a `base_url` parameter. None of these accept a real API key.
- Only `data/_teacher_client.py` actually authenticates against a frontier provider (OpenAI / Anthropic / Gemini), and it's used solely for synthetic data generation, not benchmarking.
- The `openai` SDK is already in the dependency tree (it's imported by 8 eval files and listed in `egg-info/PKG-INFO`). `anthropic>=0.88.0` is also listed in `egg-info/PKG-INFO:27`.
- No existing config key for `provider`, `base_url`, `api_key`, `api_base`, `bifrost`, or `gateway`. No `frontier` references anywhere.
- The `extra_flags` and `reasoning_parser` keys in `configs/models/cat_a/*.yaml` are present but NOT consumed by `serving/launch_vllm.sh`, suggesting that directory tree was set up for a more general loader that isn't fully wired.

## 6. Tasks, completion mode, streaming

The benchmark runs **Task A only**: a workflow-quality evaluation per `agent_benchmark.py:7-16` doc. Three composed metrics:

1. **State machine adherence** — `eval/state_accuracy.py` (`evaluate_state_machine`, parsing `[STATE: X -> Y]` annotations; `parse_state_transitions` imported at `agent_benchmark.py:25`). Per-turn and conversation-level.
2. **Tool-call F1** — `eval/tool_call_f1.py` (`evaluate_tool_calls`, `evaluate_tool_calls_conversation`, parsing `<tool_call>{...}</tool_call>` tags). Per-turn AND conversation-level (the better of the two feeds the composite, `agent_benchmark.py:683-686`).
3. **Tool chain propagation** — `eval/tool_chain_propagation.py` (`evaluate_chain_propagation`).

Composite (`compute_weighted_score` at lines 62-91): `0.4 * max(state_turn, state_seq) + 0.4 * tool_f1 + 0.2 * task_completion`. Plus `compute_full_workflow_success` (lines 94-117) gives a multiplicative success indicator.

**Completion mode** — All synchronous, single-threaded. SSE streaming is used only to measure TTFT and tool-call deltas; the loop reads chunks one at a time from a single connection. There is no concurrency across samples (`agent_benchmark.py:602` is a plain `for idx, sample in enumerate(samples)`). Stochastic trials repeat the entire dataset N times serially (`agent_benchmark.py:660-668`).

## 7. Full call graph for a single benchmark run

```
scripts/run_exp_a_single.sh (entry)
├── parse args (lines 26-67)
├── parse YAML inline (lines 69-81): model.name, inference.stochastic_trials
├── bash serving/launch_vllm.sh <config> [--kv-cache-dtype …] [--max-model-len …] [--max-num-seqs …]    [run_exp_a_single.sh:112]
│   └── serving/launch_vllm.sh
│       ├── source .venv-infer (line 28-31)
│       ├── parse_yaml() inline-Python helper (lines 48-65)
│       ├── reads model.name, serving.tool_call_parser, serving.gpu_memory_utilization,
│       │   serving.max_model_len, serving.max_num_seqs, serving.enforce_eager,
│       │   serving.port, serving.hf_overrides (lines 68-87)
│       ├── builds VLLM_ARGS array (lines 118-151)
│       └── exec one of:
│           ├── python -m llm_workflow_agents.serving.launch_vllm_turboquant   (line 172)
│           ├── python -m llm_workflow_agents.serving.launch_vllm_rotorquant   (line 175)
│           └── python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server                    (line 179)
│       → starts an HTTP server on http://localhost:8000 with --enable-auto-tool-choice
├── poll http://localhost:8000/health every 5s up to 900s   [run_exp_a_single.sh:125-135]
└── python3 -m llm_workflow_agents.eval.agent_benchmark   [line 139]
        --model $MODEL_NAME  --kv-cache-dtype …  --output … --data …
        --max-samples …  --stochastic-trials …  --log-level DEBUG
    └── src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/agent_benchmark.py
        ├── argparse (lines 507-553)
        ├── load_dotenv() (line 492) — never used downstream
        ├── _load_samples(data_dir) → list[dict]   (lines 208-220)
        ├── for each sample: _replay_conversation(endpoint, model, sample, temperature=0.0, enable_thinking)   (line 610)
        │   └── for each assistant turn: _call_vllm(endpoint, model, context, temperature, tools, enable_thinking)   (lines 422-425)
        │       ├── urllib.request.Request → POST {endpoint}/v1/chat/completions  (lines 271-276)
        │       ├── stream=True, parse SSE deltas, accumulate tool_call deltas keyed by index (lines 293-331)
        │       └── return (content_with_<tool_call>_tags, raw_tool_calls, latency_ms, ttft_ms)
        ├── for trial in stochastic_trials: replay all samples again at temperature=0.7   (lines 660-668)
        ├── evaluate_state_machine(…)   (line 674; from eval/state_accuracy.py)
        ├── evaluate_tool_calls(…) and evaluate_tool_calls_conversation(…)   (lines 675-676; from eval/tool_call_f1.py)
        ├── evaluate_chain_propagation(…)   (line 677; from eval/tool_chain_propagation.py)
        ├── evaluate_workflow_quality(…)   (lines 687-692)
        └── json.dump → $RESULT_FILE   (line 708)
```

## 8. Extension points for adding a frontier-API client

Concrete file:line locations where a frontier client could attach without rewriting the world:

1. **`agent_benchmark.py:223` — `_call_vllm` definition.** This is the single I/O surface for model completions. Any drop-in replacement that returns `(content, raw_tool_calls, latency_ms, ttft_ms)` would route the entire benchmark. The function name is misleading; nothing about its signature is vLLM-specific except the URL path and the SSE format assumption.
2. **`agent_benchmark.py:271-276` — Request construction.** Where headers are set; an `Authorization: Bearer …` could be inserted. Currently only `Content-Type`.
3. **`agent_benchmark.py:255-269` — Request body construction.** The `chat_template_kwargs`, `tools`, and `stream` keys are vLLM/OpenAI-compatible-server idioms; some frontier APIs (Anthropic) use a different shape.
4. **`agent_benchmark.py:507-553` — argparse block.** Natural place to add `--provider`, `--base-url`, `--api-key`, `--api-key-env`, `--auth-header` flags. Currently has only `--endpoint`.
5. **`agent_benchmark.py:484-492` — main entry.** `load_dotenv()` already runs but no env var is consumed; `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` could be picked up here (precedent: `data/_teacher_client.py:53,68,83`).
6. **`agent_benchmark.py:610-613` and `:664-667` — call sites of `_replay_conversation`.** If the dispatch is moved up a level (e.g. inject a "client" object), this is where the swap happens.
7. **`scripts/run_exp_a_single.sh:139-147` — CLI invocation.** New flags added to `agent_benchmark.py` need plumbing here. Currently the script ignores `--endpoint` entirely.
8. **`scripts/run_exp_a_single.sh:69-81` — YAML readers.** Pattern for extracting new config keys (e.g. `provider`, `base_url`, `api_key_env`) and passing them to the harness.
9. **`scripts/run_exp_a_single.sh:112-135` — vLLM server lifecycle.** For a frontier provider this entire block would be skipped; the cleanup trap (line 115-120) and health-poll loop are vLLM-specific.
10. **`src/llm_workflow_agents/config/schema.py:44-52` — `ServingConfig`.** Adding `provider`, `base_url`, `api_key_env`, `headers` fields to this Pydantic model would let `configs/models/cat_a/*.yaml` configs declare a frontier endpoint without touching the loader. Note: this schema is not consumed by the current `run_exp_a*.sh` flow (which uses inline YAML reads), so wiring through `loader.py:25-33` would also be needed.
11. **`src/llm_workflow_agents/data/_teacher_client.py:18-42`** — existing `call_teacher_model()` provider-routing function. Pattern (prefix-based dispatch on model name to `_call_openai` / `_call_anthropic` / `_call_gemini`) is the closest in-repo template for adding multi-provider support, but it lacks streaming, tool-calling, TTFT measurement, and conversation-shaped messages. Re-using it directly would require substantial extension.
12. **`src/llm_workflow_agents/serving/orchestrator.py:62`** — `self.base_url = orchestrator_config.get("base_url", "http://localhost:8000/v1")`. Already supports a configurable `base_url` from a config dict. Closest in-repo example of YAML-driven endpoint routing, though still hardcodes `api_key="unused"` at line 134.

## 9. Notable gotchas

- `agent_benchmark.py:265` always sends `chat_template_kwargs={"enable_thinking": …}`. Frontier APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini) will reject unknown body fields — this would need to be guarded behind a vLLM-only branch.
- `agent_benchmark.py:268` conditionally adds `tools=[…]`. Anthropic uses `tools` too but with a different schema (`input_schema` instead of OpenAI's `function.parameters`). The schemas in `sample["tool_schemas"]` are OpenAI-shaped (function/parameters), confirmed by `tool_metrics_conversation` evaluation logic.
- `_call_vllm` returns `raw_tool_calls` as the OpenAI-format list `{id, type:"function", function:{name, arguments}}` (lines 318-323). Downstream code at `agent_benchmark.py:441-448` uses this `id` to populate `tool_call_id` on subsequent `tool` messages — any client that doesn't surface tool-call IDs in the OpenAI shape will break the multi-turn replay.
- The `--kv-cache-dtype` arg flows into result JSON metadata only (line 700), not into the model request — but result-file naming logic at `run_exp_a_single.sh:138` uses it, so a frontier run would need a non-vLLM-flavored stand-in value.
- The `chat_template` YAML key is purely descriptive; no code reads it. vLLM uses the tokenizer's built-in template by default. There's no current mechanism to inject a custom chat template into the harness.
- Both `models_exp_a/` and `models/cat_a/` directories exist with overlapping but non-identical schemas. `run_exp_a*.sh` only uses `models_exp_a/`; `models/cat_a/` is loaded by `config/loader.py` but I found no script that actually invokes that loader for benchmarking.

I have enough information to compile the report.

---

# Phase 1 Benchmark Harness Research Report

## 1. File contents (verbatim)

### 1a. `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/scripts/run_exp_a_single.sh`
Already shown in full above (151 lines). Key shape:
- Lines 1-29: usage banner / arg parsing scaffold
- Lines 31-67: positional config arg + flag parsing (`--kv-cache-dtype`, `--data`, `--max-samples`, `--max-model-len`, `--max-num-seqs`, `--dry-run`); resolves config to absolute path
- Lines 69-81: inline-Python YAML readers extracting `model.name` and `inference.stochastic_trials`
- Lines 97-103: builds vLLM launch arg array
- Lines 112-135: `bash $LAUNCH_SCRIPT … &` then polls `http://localhost:8000/health` for up to 900s; HARDCODED to localhost:8000 for the readiness gate
- Lines 138-147: invokes `python3 -m llm_workflow_agents.eval.agent_benchmark` with `--model`, `--kv-cache-dtype`, `--output`, `--data`, `--max-samples`, `--stochastic-trials`, `--log-level DEBUG`. **Does NOT pass `--endpoint`** — relies on the harness default `http://localhost:8000`. Does NOT pass an API key.

### 1b. `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/scripts/run_exp_a.sh`
Already shown in full above (126 lines). Same structure, but loops over a hardcoded list of 10 YAMLs in `MODEL_CONFIGS=(…)` (lines 43-54) and only forwards `--kv-cache-dtype` to the launch script (line 89). Also does NOT forward `--endpoint`.

No other `run_exp_a*.sh` siblings exist (only those two).

### 1c. `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/agent_benchmark.py`
Already shown in full above (720 lines). Critical zones:
- Lines 33-59: `WorkflowQualityMetrics` dataclass
- Lines 62-201: pure metric-computation helpers (no I/O)
- Lines 208-220: `_load_samples` reads JSONL files
- Lines 223-354: `_call_vllm(endpoint, model, messages, temperature, max_tokens, tools, enable_thinking)` — the **single I/O surface** to the model. Hand-rolled `urllib.request` POST to `{endpoint}/v1/chat/completions` with `stream=True`. Parses SSE deltas, accumulates tool_call deltas keyed by `index`, measures TTFT and end-to-end latency, appends `<tool_call>{...}</tool_call>` tags into the returned content for downstream regex parsing
- Lines 357-481: `_replay_conversation(endpoint, model, sample, temperature, enable_thinking)` — replays a conversation with substituted assistant turns; preserves ground-truth tool responses; assigns `tool_call_id` from the model's prior tool_call list
- Lines 484-720: `if __name__ == "__main__":` CLI block

### Key CLI args (lines 507-553)
- `--model` (required) — string passed verbatim into `request_body["model"]`
- `--output` (required) — path
- `--data` — default `data/output/benchmark/task_a`
- `--endpoint` — default `http://localhost:8000` (line 517-519). This is the ONLY existing knob for routing.
- `--max-samples` int default 0
- `--stochastic-trials` int default 5
- `--kv-cache-dtype` string, only used for result metadata (recorded in JSON, NOT forwarded into requests)
- `--log-level` choice
- `--enable-thinking` bool flag

There is no `--api-key`, `--provider`, `--base-url`, `--tool-call-parser`, or `--chat-template` CLI arg.

## 2. How the harness obtains a model client

- The harness does **not** spawn vLLM. The shell script (`run_exp_a_single.sh:112`) backgrounds `serving/launch_vllm.sh`, polls `/health`, then invokes the Python harness.
- The Python harness opens an HTTP connection on every assistant turn via raw `urllib.request` (lines 251-353). It hits the **OpenAI-compatible** `/v1/chat/completions` route exposed by `vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server`.
- It is **synchronous and streaming** (SSE) — `stream=True` body (line 260), single-thread loop reading line-by-line. No `asyncio`, no `openai` SDK, no `requests`, no auth headers.
- Hardcoded request shape: `model`, `messages`, `temperature`, `max_tokens`, `stream`, `chat_template_kwargs={enable_thinking: bool}`, optional `tools`. **No `Authorization` header is ever set** (line 274 only sets `Content-Type`).

## 3. Where model name / base URL / API key / tool_call_parser / chat_template are wired in

| Setting | Where it lives | How it reaches the model |
|---|---|---|
| `model.name` | YAML model config (e.g. `configs/models_exp_a/gemma3_27b.yaml:2`) | Read by `run_exp_a_single.sh:69-74` via inline Python; passed as `--model` to `agent_benchmark.py` (`run_exp_a_single.sh:140`); placed verbatim into the request body at `agent_benchmark.py:257` |
| Base URL | NOT in any YAML. Only `--endpoint` CLI arg (`agent_benchmark.py:516-519`) defaults to `http://localhost:8000`. The shell script never overrides this. The vLLM port itself can be set via `serving.port` YAML key (`launch_vllm.sh:74`) or `--port` flag, but the eval harness has no symmetric override and is hardwired to `localhost:8000` |
| API key | **NOT WIRED ANYWHERE** in this code path. `_call_vllm` (`agent_benchmark.py:271-276`) sets only `Content-Type: application/json`. `dotenv.load_dotenv()` is called at `agent_benchmark.py:492` but no env var is consumed |
| `tool_call_parser` | YAML `serving.tool_call_parser` (e.g. `gemma3_27b.yaml:13`); read by `launch_vllm.sh:69`; passed to vLLM as `--tool-call-parser` (`launch_vllm.sh:121`). Server-side only; the harness never sees it |
| `chat_template` | YAML `serving.chat_template` (e.g. `gemma3_27b.yaml:14`, `qwen3_32b.yaml:14`). **NEVER consumed.** No `grep -rn chat_template` produced any read of this key — `launch_vllm.sh` doesn't reference it, vLLM uses the tokenizer's built-in template by default. It is purely metadata |
| `enable_thinking` | Only the Python harness CLI flag `--enable-thinking` (`agent_benchmark.py:544-552`) → `chat_template_kwargs` body field (`agent_benchmark.py:265`). Not in YAML |
| Other vLLM serving knobs (`gpu_memory_utilization`, `max_model_len`, `enforce_eager`, `max_num_seqs`, `hf_overrides`) | YAML `serving.*` → `launch_vllm.sh:67-87` → server CLI args. Server-side only |
| `extra_flags` (Phase 1B configs only) | Listed in `configs/models/cat_a/qwen35_35b_a3b.yaml:19-21` and Qwen3.6 configs. NOT consumed by `serving/launch_vllm.sh` (no grep hits in launch script) — these YAMLs appear to use a different, unimplemented serving wrapper |
| `revision` (cat_a configs) | Listed in `configs/models/cat_a/qwen3_32b.yaml:3` etc. NOT consumed by `serving/launch_vllm.sh` |

## 4. Phase 1 YAML config schemas

Two parallel directory trees, with subtly different shapes:

### `configs/models_exp_a/*.yaml` (used by `run_exp_a*.sh`)
Representative — `configs/models_exp_a/gemma3_27b.yaml` (full, 28 lines):
```yaml
model:
  name: "google/gemma-3-27b-it"
  family: "gemma"
  architecture: "dense_gqa_transformer"
  params_total: 27_000_000_000
  params_active: 27_000_000_000
  context_length: 131072
  precision: "bfloat16"
  vram_estimate_gb: 54

serving:
  engine: "vllm"
  tool_call_parser: "pythonic"
  chat_template: "gemma"
  gpu_memory_utilization: 0.90
  max_model_len: 8192
  enforce_eager: false
  hf_overrides:
    rope_scaling: null

inference:
  temperature_deterministic: 0.0
  temperature_stochastic: 0.7
  stochastic_trials: 5
  max_tokens: 2048
```

`configs/models_exp_a/qwen3_32b.yaml` (full, 24 lines): same shape, no `hf_overrides`, `chat_template: "chatml"`, `tool_call_parser: "hermes"`.

### `configs/models/cat_a/*.yaml` (newer / not directly wired into `run_exp_a.sh`)
`configs/models/cat_a/qwen3_32b.yaml` (full, 23 lines):
```yaml
model:
  name: "Qwen/Qwen3-32B"
  revision: "9216db5781bf21249d130ec9da846c4624c16137"
  family: "qwen"
  …
serving:
  engine: "vllm"
  tool_call_parser: "hermes"
  chat_template: "chatml"
  gpu_memory_utilization: 0.90
  max_model_len: 8192
category: "A"
benchmark_tasks:
  - "task_a"
```

`configs/models/cat_a/qwen35_35b_a3b.yaml:12-21` (extended schema with `extra_flags` and `reasoning_parser`):
```yaml
serving:
  engine: "vllm"
  tool_call_parser: "qwen3_coder"
  reasoning_parser: "qwen3"
  chat_template: "chatml"
  gpu_memory_utilization: 0.90
  max_model_len: 8192
  extra_flags:
    - "--enable-auto-tool-choice"
    - "--language-model-only"
```

The Pydantic schema for these YAMLs lives at `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/src/llm_workflow_agents/config/schema.py:44-79`. `ServingConfig` (lines 44-52) only covers: `engine`, `tool_call_parser`, `chat_template`, `gpu_memory_utilization`, `max_model_len`, `enforce_eager`. `reasoning_parser`, `extra_flags`, `revision`, `hf_overrides` are NOT in the Pydantic model — they would either be silently dropped or rejected on validation (by default Pydantic BaseModel allows extras only if not configured; these models don't set `model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")`, so extras pass silently).

Loader: `/workspaces/ai-agent-llms/src/llm_workflow_agents/config/loader.py:25-33` `load_model_config` — the harness does NOT use this loader; the shell script reads YAML inline.

## 5. Existing remote / OpenAI-compatible / frontier code paths

| Path | File:lines | Mode | Reusable for frontier API? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Hand-rolled urllib SSE → `{endpoint}/v1/chat/completions` (Phase 1 agent benchmark) | `eval/agent_benchmark.py:223-354` | sync streaming, no auth | The TARGET of the question. Currently ONLY supports unauth localhost vLLM |
| Hand-rolled urllib SSE for concurrency probing | `eval/concurrency_benchmark.py:185-248` (with `--base-url` CLI arg at line 488) | async via thread-pool, no auth | Pattern, but no auth |
| `openai.OpenAI(base_url=…, api_key="unused")` for needle-in-haystack | `eval/needle_haystack.py:111, 125, 132` | sync via openai SDK | Already uses the openai SDK pattern. Hardcoded `api_key="unused"` |
| `openai.OpenAI(base_url=…, api_key="unused")` for LongBench | `eval/longbench.py:180, 255, 263` | sync via openai SDK | Same pattern as needle |
| `openai.OpenAI(base_url=…, api_key="unused")` for constrained-decoding eval | `eval/constrained_decoding.py:126, 149` | sync via openai SDK | Same pattern |
| `openai.OpenAI(base_url=…, api_key="unused")` for intent classification | `eval/intent_classification.py:306, 354` | sync via openai SDK | Same pattern |
| `openai.OpenAI(base_url=…, api_key="unused")` for perplexity-vllm | `eval/perplexity.py:78, 155` | sync via openai SDK | Same pattern |
| `openai.OpenAI(base_url=…, api_key="unused")` for quant_benchmark | `eval/quant_benchmark.py:255` | sync via openai SDK | Same pattern |
| `openai.AsyncOpenAI(base_url=…, api_key="unused")` for E2E benchmark | `serving/benchmark_e2e.py:128, 156, 158` | async openai SDK | Same pattern, async |
| `openai.AsyncOpenAI(base_url=…, api_key="unused")` for orchestrator | `serving/orchestrator.py:62, 132, 134` | async openai SDK | Same pattern, async; `base_url` taken from `orchestrator_config["base_url"]` (configurable) |
| Frontier-provider teacher client (data generation, NOT eval) | `data/_teacher_client.py:1-94` | sync; routes by model-name prefix to `openai.OpenAI`, `anthropic.Anthropic`, or `google.genai`; reads `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` from env via `dotenv` | **Closest existing analogue.** Already implements provider routing pattern, but: (a) no streaming, (b) no `tools`/`tool_calls` support, (c) returns plain text only, not OpenAI message objects |

**Findings on existing remote/openai-compatible pathways**

- The codebase already standardises on the OpenAI-compatible Chat Completions wire format almost everywhere except `agent_benchmark.py` (which uses raw urllib).
- 8 different eval modules use `openai.OpenAI(base_url=…, api_key="unused")` and accept a `base_url` parameter. None of these accept a real API key.
- Only `data/_teacher_client.py` actually authenticates against a frontier provider (OpenAI / Anthropic / Gemini), and it's used solely for synthetic data generation, not benchmarking.
- The `openai` SDK is already in the dependency tree (it's imported by 8 eval files and listed in `egg-info/PKG-INFO`). `anthropic>=0.88.0` is also listed in `egg-info/PKG-INFO:27`.
- No existing config key for `provider`, `base_url`, `api_key`, `api_base`, `bifrost`, or `gateway`. No `frontier` references anywhere.
- The `extra_flags` and `reasoning_parser` keys in `configs/models/cat_a/*.yaml` are present but NOT consumed by `serving/launch_vllm.sh`, suggesting that directory tree was set up for a more general loader that isn't fully wired.

## 6. Tasks, completion mode, streaming

The benchmark runs **Task A only**: a workflow-quality evaluation per `agent_benchmark.py:7-16` doc. Three composed metrics:

1. **State machine adherence** — `eval/state_accuracy.py` (`evaluate_state_machine`, parsing `[STATE: X -> Y]` annotations; `parse_state_transitions` imported at `agent_benchmark.py:25`). Per-turn and conversation-level.
2. **Tool-call F1** — `eval/tool_call_f1.py` (`evaluate_tool_calls`, `evaluate_tool_calls_conversation`, parsing `<tool_call>{...}</tool_call>` tags). Per-turn AND conversation-level (the better of the two feeds the composite, `agent_benchmark.py:683-686`).
3. **Tool chain propagation** — `eval/tool_chain_propagation.py` (`evaluate_chain_propagation`).

Composite (`compute_weighted_score` at lines 62-91): `0.4 * max(state_turn, state_seq) + 0.4 * tool_f1 + 0.2 * task_completion`. Plus `compute_full_workflow_success` (lines 94-117) gives a multiplicative success indicator.

**Completion mode** — All synchronous, single-threaded. SSE streaming is used only to measure TTFT and tool-call deltas; the loop reads chunks one at a time from a single connection. There is no concurrency across samples (`agent_benchmark.py:602` is a plain `for idx, sample in enumerate(samples)`). Stochastic trials repeat the entire dataset N times serially (`agent_benchmark.py:660-668`).

## 7. Full call graph for a single benchmark run

```
scripts/run_exp_a_single.sh (entry)
├── parse args (lines 26-67)
├── parse YAML inline (lines 69-81): model.name, inference.stochastic_trials
├── bash serving/launch_vllm.sh <config> [--kv-cache-dtype …] [--max-model-len …] [--max-num-seqs …]    [run_exp_a_single.sh:112]
│   └── serving/launch_vllm.sh
│       ├── source .venv-infer (line 28-31)
│       ├── parse_yaml() inline-Python helper (lines 48-65)
│       ├── reads model.name, serving.tool_call_parser, serving.gpu_memory_utilization,
│       │   serving.max_model_len, serving.max_num_seqs, serving.enforce_eager,
│       │   serving.port, serving.hf_overrides (lines 68-87)
│       ├── builds VLLM_ARGS array (lines 118-151)
│       └── exec one of:
│           ├── python -m llm_workflow_agents.serving.launch_vllm_turboquant   (line 172)
│           ├── python -m llm_workflow_agents.serving.launch_vllm_rotorquant   (line 175)
│           └── python -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server                    (line 179)
│       → starts an HTTP server on http://localhost:8000 with --enable-auto-tool-choice
├── poll http://localhost:8000/health every 5s up to 900s   [run_exp_a_single.sh:125-135]
└── python3 -m llm_workflow_agents.eval.agent_benchmark   [line 139]
        --model $MODEL_NAME  --kv-cache-dtype …  --output … --data …
        --max-samples …  --stochastic-trials …  --log-level DEBUG
    └── src/llm_workflow_agents/eval/agent_benchmark.py
        ├── argparse (lines 507-553)
        ├── load_dotenv() (line 492) — never used downstream
        ├── _load_samples(data_dir) → list[dict]   (lines 208-220)
        ├── for each sample: _replay_conversation(endpoint, model, sample, temperature=0.0, enable_thinking)   (line 610)
        │   └── for each assistant turn: _call_vllm(endpoint, model, context, temperature, tools, enable_thinking)   (lines 422-425)
        │       ├── urllib.request.Request → POST {endpoint}/v1/chat/completions  (lines 271-276)
        │       ├── stream=True, parse SSE deltas, accumulate tool_call deltas keyed by index (lines 293-331)
        │       └── return (content_with_<tool_call>_tags, raw_tool_calls, latency_ms, ttft_ms)
        ├── for trial in stochastic_trials: replay all samples again at temperature=0.7   (lines 660-668)
        ├── evaluate_state_machine(…)   (line 674; from eval/state_accuracy.py)
        ├── evaluate_tool_calls(…) and evaluate_tool_calls_conversation(…)   (lines 675-676; from eval/tool_call_f1.py)
        ├── evaluate_chain_propagation(…)   (line 677; from eval/tool_chain_propagation.py)
        ├── evaluate_workflow_quality(…)   (lines 687-692)
        └── json.dump → $RESULT_FILE   (line 708)
```

## 8. Extension points for adding a frontier-API client

Concrete file:line locations where a frontier client could attach without rewriting the world:

1. **`agent_benchmark.py:223` — `_call_vllm` definition.** This is the single I/O surface for model completions. Any drop-in replacement that returns `(content, raw_tool_calls, latency_ms, ttft_ms)` would route the entire benchmark. The function name is misleading; nothing about its signature is vLLM-specific except the URL path and the SSE format assumption.
2. **`agent_benchmark.py:271-276` — Request construction.** Where headers are set; an `Authorization: Bearer …` could be inserted. Currently only `Content-Type`.
3. **`agent_benchmark.py:255-269` — Request body construction.** The `chat_template_kwargs`, `tools`, and `stream` keys are vLLM/OpenAI-compatible-server idioms; some frontier APIs (Anthropic) use a different shape.
4. **`agent_benchmark.py:507-553` — argparse block.** Natural place to add `--provider`, `--base-url`, `--api-key`, `--api-key-env`, `--auth-header` flags. Currently has only `--endpoint`.
5. **`agent_benchmark.py:484-492` — main entry.** `load_dotenv()` already runs but no env var is consumed; `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` could be picked up here (precedent: `data/_teacher_client.py:53,68,83`).
6. **`agent_benchmark.py:610-613` and `:664-667` — call sites of `_replay_conversation`.** If the dispatch is moved up a level (e.g. inject a "client" object), this is where the swap happens.
7. **`scripts/run_exp_a_single.sh:139-147` — CLI invocation.** New flags added to `agent_benchmark.py` need plumbing here. Currently the script ignores `--endpoint` entirely.
8. **`scripts/run_exp_a_single.sh:69-81` — YAML readers.** Pattern for extracting new config keys (e.g. `provider`, `base_url`, `api_key_env`) and passing them to the harness.
9. **`scripts/run_exp_a_single.sh:112-135` — vLLM server lifecycle.** For a frontier provider this entire block would be skipped; the cleanup trap (line 115-120) and health-poll loop are vLLM-specific.
10. **`src/llm_workflow_agents/config/schema.py:44-52` — `ServingConfig`.** Adding `provider`, `base_url`, `api_key_env`, `headers` fields to this Pydantic model would let `configs/models/cat_a/*.yaml` configs declare a frontier endpoint without touching the loader. Note: this schema is not consumed by the current `run_exp_a*.sh` flow (which uses inline YAML reads), so wiring through `loader.py:25-33` would also be needed.
11. **`src/llm_workflow_agents/data/_teacher_client.py:18-42`** — existing `call_teacher_model()` provider-routing function. Pattern (prefix-based dispatch on model name to `_call_openai` / `_call_anthropic` / `_call_gemini`) is the closest in-repo template for adding multi-provider support, but it lacks streaming, tool-calling, TTFT measurement, and conversation-shaped messages. Re-using it directly would require substantial extension.
12. **`src/llm_workflow_agents/serving/orchestrator.py:62`** — `self.base_url = orchestrator_config.get("base_url", "http://localhost:8000/v1")`. Already supports a configurable `base_url` from a config dict. Closest in-repo example of YAML-driven endpoint routing, though still hardcodes `api_key="unused"` at line 134.

## 9. Notable gotchas

- `agent_benchmark.py:265` always sends `chat_template_kwargs={"enable_thinking": …}`. Frontier APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini) will reject unknown body fields — this would need to be guarded behind a vLLM-only branch.
- `agent_benchmark.py:268` conditionally adds `tools=[…]`. Anthropic uses `tools` too but with a different schema (`input_schema` instead of OpenAI's `function.parameters`). The schemas in `sample["tool_schemas"]` are OpenAI-shaped (function/parameters), confirmed by `tool_metrics_conversation` evaluation logic.
- `_call_vllm` returns `raw_tool_calls` as the OpenAI-format list `{id, type:"function", function:{name, arguments}}` (lines 318-323). Downstream code at `agent_benchmark.py:441-448` uses this `id` to populate `tool_call_id` on subsequent `tool` messages — any client that doesn't surface tool-call IDs in the OpenAI shape will break the multi-turn replay.
- The `--kv-cache-dtype` arg flows into result JSON metadata only (line 700), not into the model request — but result-file naming logic at `run_exp_a_single.sh:138` uses it, so a frontier run would need a non-vLLM-flavored stand-in value.
- The `chat_template` YAML key is purely descriptive; no code reads it. vLLM uses the tokenizer's built-in template by default. There's no current mechanism to inject a custom chat template into the harness.
- Both `models_exp_a/` and `models/cat_a/` directories exist with overlapping but non-identical schemas. `run_exp_a*.sh` only uses `models_exp_a/`; `models/cat_a/` is loaded by `config/loader.py` but I found no script that actually invokes that loader for benchmarking.

lastPrompt: LLM API will be served via this gateway. Edit `run_exp_a_single.sh` script and benchmark logic such that it can run benchmark on any frontier model specified in `deployments/local/data/bifrost/config.…

aiTitle: Start Docker Compose deployment for AI agent

content.text: I have a clear picture of the current setup. Before writing the final plan, I want to confirm a few design choices.

questions.question: How should frontier models be selected when invoking the benchmark?

options.label: Single template + --model flag (Recommended)
options.description: One shared YAML (e.g. configs/models_exp_a/frontier.yaml) holding bench/inference defaults. Caller passes --model gpt-5 (or claude-sonnet-4-6 etc.) to run_exp_a_single.sh. Script validates that model exists in bifrost/config.json. No file sprawl when bifrost config changes.

options.label: One YAML per frontier model
options.description: Generate configs/models_exp_a/frontier/<model>.yaml for each of the 13 models in bifrost/config.json. Mirrors existing per-model YAML pattern. Adds 13+ files; need regeneration whenever bifrost config changes.

options.label: Both: per-model YAMLs auto-generated from bifrost/config.json
options.description: Provide a small generator script (e.g. scripts/sync_frontier_configs.py) that reads bifrost/config.json and writes one YAML per model into configs/models_exp_a/frontier/. Caller invokes script normally. Re-run generator to refresh.

questions.question: Should run_exp_a.sh (the bulk runner that loops over multiple models) also be updated to support frontier models?

options.label: Yes — extend both scripts
options.description: Update run_exp_a.sh so the MODEL_CONFIGS list can include frontier YAMLs and frontier rows skip vLLM launch. Lets you run all frontier models (or a mix of vLLM + frontier) in one batch.

options.label: No — only run_exp_a_single.sh
options.description: Stick to the single-model runner only. Bulk runs of frontier models can be done via a shell loop later.

questions.question: How should we keep frontier API costs predictable during the run?

options.label: Lower default --max-samples + --stochastic-trials for frontier (Recommended)
options.description: When the resolved engine is bifrost, default to e.g. --max-samples 50 and --stochastic-trials 2 (still overridable via CLI). vLLM behaviour unchanged.

options.label: Keep current defaults (full dataset, 5 trials)
options.description: Same defaults as vLLM runs — full benchmark, ~13 frontier models × full dataset × 5 trials. Highest fidelity; user pays the bill.

options.label: Add a --budget-usd hard stop
options.description: Estimate cost from token counts and abort early if a configurable budget is exceeded. More work; needs per-model pricing table.
